This quarantine has made me realize how much I deeply value the podcast Harmontown, which has been a bedrock of my sanity in this trying time. It is extraordinarily difficult to explain to people why I love it so much. Might make a podcast about it. Here’s my mindmap. https://t.co/0dWZ0jApkK
private email discussion list now functional
@mbgerring I appreciate your positivity and all that you do.

@mbgerring Coming from a place of wanting to help people, of knowing I am only as strong as my community, that we are stronger together – it is such an insane slap in the face when people disregard their own health (OUR HEALTH) for politics. How to help people who won't help themselves?

@mbgerring Totally agree, but with that said– I have been profoundly surprised at how much basic survival has overwhelmed what I thought were reasonably intelligent human beings I share this earth with

@mbgerring I've collected over 50 examples of mutual aid and people helping each other during the Coronavirus https://t.co/fx6HJUp45L and I still feel profoundly disappointed in my fellow human beings. I am proud of these people, of course, but they were already my friends and allies.

@mbgerring I have seen a lot of casual stupidity just in person in New York- people who are trying but are clearly unaware of how viruses actually spread, people not trying at all, others inundating remote communities with fragile health care systems, this shit is deadly dumb

@mbgerring Sure, but frankly, those things are coming from the people I expected them from. I would have expected a lot more coming together, a lot more cooperating, we haven't even come close to the brief unity I remember feeling after 9/11.
The same, I suppose should be said for our enemies – they don't want to confuse us, they don't want us distracted– they want us dead.

This has been my main realization from this whole global pandemic, I did not have a particularly high expectation for the intelligence of my fellow humans, but even then, it has been clear that more people are fatally stupid  than I was giving them credit for my whole life https://t.co/f1oRfuFk6Y
As much as I love their ridiculously ludicrously crass articles.
